policies.reason: Reported sinks are guarded by strict segment validation and path boundary enforcement (PathConfig, resolveSafePath, sanitizePlatformSegment, language/platform regex checks).

match.messageRegex: ^This route handler performs \[(authorization|a file system access)\].*not rate-limited\.$

policies.reason: Routes are protected by explicit rate-limit middleware and auth middleware, but static analysis can miss middleware chaining across router definitions.

match.messageRegex: ^Helmet security middleware, configured with security setting \[contentSecurityPolicy\] set to 'false'.*$

policies.reason: CSP is disabled only in development mode for tooling compatibility; production branch enables CSP directives.

match.messageRegex: ^The \[URL\] of this request depends on a \[user-provided value\].*$

policies.reason: Outbound requests use hardcoded vendor API hosts; user input only affects post/resource IDs, not destination host/protocol.

match.messageRegex: ^This replacement may produce '&' characters that are double-unescaped.*$

policies.reason: Functions are best-effort text extraction helpers for preview/plain text, not trust-boundary HTML sanitizers before injection into executable contexts.

match.messageRegex: ^This regular expression does not match script end tags like </script >\.$

policies.reason: Regexes are used for formatting cleanup in rendering helpers; these code paths do not claim complete XSS sanitization guarantees.

match.messageRegex: ^This string may still contain \[<.*\], which may cause an HTML element injection vulnerability\.$

policies.reason: Findings target content transformation and preview composition helpers; they are not used as a sole security sanitizer at an untrusted DOM sink.
Matched value is platform metadata string ('linkedin' module identifier), not a credential, token, or client secret.
